questions.question: Define packages in Java and explain their importance in organizing related classes and interfaces.
keyPoints: Definition of packages
Importance of packages in organizing related classes
Avoiding naming conflicts

questions.question: Explain briefly the difference between wildcard import (import java.util.*;) and specific import (import java.util.ArrayList;) in Java.
keyPoints: Wildcard import definition
Specific import definition
Key differences between wildcard and specific import

questions.question: Explain with examples how packages are used to encapsulate and protect data in a Java program, including the use of access modifiers such as public, private, and protected.
keyPoints: Definition of encapsulation and data protection
Use of access modifiers (public, private, protected)
Example of encapsulation using packages and access modifiers
Benefits of encapsulation in building robust and secure applications

questions.question: Compare and contrast the use of built-in packages (e.g., java.lang, java.util) and user-defined packages in Java programming.
keyPoints: Definition of built-in packages
Definition of user-defined packages
Key differences between built-in and user-defined packages
Example usage of built-in and user-defined packages
Advantages and disadvantages of using built-in and user-defined packages

questions.question: Discuss the advantages of using packages in Java programming, including namespace management, access control, and modularization.
keyPoints: Definition of namespace management
Definition of access control
Definition of modularization
Example of how packages achieve namespace management, access control, and modularization
Benefits of using packages in large-scale Java applications
